body.title: session_capture.py: drop dead adc.palace writes, keep flat-file session record
body.description:
**Problem:** scripts/session_capture.py (called by the savestate skill's session-close path) writes a transcript + summary node via psycopg2 INTO the retired `adc.palace` table (INSERT at line 34, SELECT at 104) AND writes flat-file echoes to ~/.unseen_university/claudecode/palace_echo/. The palace table is dead (flat-file store is canonical), so the psql write either errors or writes to a table nothing reads — a dead interface crossing on every session close.
**Proposed shape:** Strip the psycopg2 / adc.palace path entirely (the INSERT, the LIKE SELECT for numbering, the psycopg2 imports/connect). Keep the session record as a FLAT-FILE write — either the existing palace_echo flat-file (renamed to a non-'palace' path under the canonical store) or routed through the canonical session writer (memory_emit.py / session_store under devlab/runtime/memory/sessions/). Decide at sprint time which flat-file home is canonical for session transcripts+summaries (check what /context-load and day-close actually read for sessions) and write there — do not invent a new ~/.unseen_university path without confirming (CLAUDE.md). Then confirm the savestate skill's call still succeeds end-to-end. NOTE the script also moves to its correct home is OUT of scope here — keep it at scripts/session_capture.py for now (or fold a move into a later pass); this ticket is the dead-write fix only.
**Affected files:** scripts/session_capture.py (remove psql/palace, keep/repoint flat-file write); possibly skills/savestate/SKILL.md or run (if the call signature/behavior changes); session record home under devlab/runtime/memory/sessions/ (if routing there).
**Design rules:** filesystem memory store is canonical (no palace); flat-file not Postgres for this path; memory_emit.py is the write chokepoint if routing into the store; check with Akien before any NEW ~/.unseen_university path (reuse an existing canonical home); log the interface crossing (session write) at INFO.
**Scope boundary:** IN — remove the dead adc.palace psql writes from session_capture; ensure a flat-file session record is written to a canonical home; verify savestate's call works. OUT — relocating session_capture.py out of scripts/; redesigning session-record schema; the other dead scripts (separate ticket).
**Test plan:** (1) `grep -nE 'psycopg|adc\.palace' scripts/session_capture.py` returns 0. (2) Run `python3 scripts/session_capture.py --dry-run` (or the savestate close path) and assert it writes a flat-file session record (path printed/exists) with NO psycopg2 connection attempted. (3) A savestate session-close produces a readable flat-file session record, not a psql error. (4) The record is in a canonical home a reader (context-load/day-close) already consults, or the reason it is standalone is noted.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.result: Stripped dead adc.palace psql INSERT (psycopg2 connect, UPSERT, slot SELECT, palace_echo) from session_capture.py; session record now writes one node to canonical devlab/runtime/memory/sessions/ via memory_emit, logged at INFO. Red->green proof (commit e146c4cc). grep psycopg|adc.palace = 0; savestate no-arg invocation + dry-run still work; savestate test passes.
